content:
EXECUTE REACT NATIVE WORKSPACE MIGRATION NOW

The current Rork workspace was incorrectly created as Swift and cannot display my Expo/React Native application in the built-in emulator.

Create a NEW Rork workspace with platform type:

React Native / Expo

Then import my existing GitHub repository:

@[github:https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fibb142%2Frork-global-real-estate-invest:GitHub Sync - ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest]

Use this application root:

/expo

NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENTS:

1. Do not create a blank replacement app.
2. Do not copy only selected screens.
3. Import the complete existing Expo application.
4. Preserve all routes, screens, assets, authentication, owner login, Supabase configuration, Render API configuration, and environment-variable names.
5. My GitHub repository must remain the source of truth.
6. Do not move the code to a Rork-owned repository.
7. Do not change production credentials.
8. Do not ask for credentials unless an actual runtime request returns missing, expired, revoked, 401, or 403.

EXECUTE:

A. Create the new workspace as React Native/Expo.
B. Connect it to ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest.
C. Set /expo as the working project root.
D. Install dependencies from the existing lockfile.
E. Run Expo diagnostics.
F. Start Metro.
G. Register the app with the Rork emulator.
H. Launch the real IVX app in Android emulator.
I. Launch the real IVX app in iOS emulator if supported.
J. Verify owner sign-in, navigation, Supabase, and Render API.
K. Push only necessary fixes back to the same GitHub repository.

REQUIRED PROOF:

1. New Rork workspace URL
2. Workspace platform type showing React Native
3. Connected GitHub repository
4. Active branch
5. Exact project root
6. Final commit SHA
7. Metro successful bundle output
8. Android emulator screenshot showing the real IVX app
9. iOS emulator screenshot if supported
10. Owner sign-in test result
11. Render /health HTTP status
12. Supabase connection result
13. Final status:
   VERIFIED_COMPLETE
   or
   BLOCKED_WITH_EXACT_ERROR

Do not stop at instructions. Create the correct workspace, import the repository, run the application, test it, and provide evidence.
